item.title: What Happened Today #101
item.description: ? While Qatar gifts Trump a $400 million flying palace and China buys tariff relief, everything in America is for sale except mercy for a mother torn from her child or healthcare for those who can't afford $35 copays. Foreign powers write checks while judges are terrorized at home and families are destroyed in the streets. This isn't governance; it's an auction where your rights are the currency. Don't rely on the algorithm. Let me send this to you every night, free and private, so that you never miss what's going on. Add your email on the homepage of https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/china-tariffs-suspendedit 2. https://go.govbrief.today/us-iran-talks 3. https://go.govbrief.today/qatar-747 4. https://go.govbrief.today/hamas-hostage-release 5. https://go.govbrief.today/drug-pricing-order 6. https://go.govbrief.today/gop-cuts-medicaid 7. https://go.govbrief.today/judges-home-orders 8. https://go.govbrief.today/newark-flights-reduced 9. https://go.govbrief.today/worcester-protest-day-three
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Bottom shelf memes that are cheaper than therapy.
item.description:
More stolen content from our own @JonathanDurham
@HileyNoteson Yoooooooooooooooooooooo
Recently, I stumbled across a bottomshelfofficial website. Turns out, there's a death metal band in Wisconsin named Bottom Shelf, so I reached out to introduce myself and invited them to game with me in the future. I certainly wasn't actually expecting a reply, but to my surprise, they responded.
Do you love memes but hate ads? Check out discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka
Potato and Peanut tax. You can catch them live on twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka fucking up my gameplay.
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.dc:creator: DOcelot1

item.title: I'm not going to say I'm a true believer, but I think it's on to something
item.description: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dead_Internet_theory TL;DR follows: Many years ago, I was channel-surfing and came upon a reality show whose premise was people with bizarre addictions, or something. The segment featured a man who was supposedly in love with his car, sexually. Lots of heartfelt narration, and clips to camera of him "making out" with his car. It was, to put it mildly, unconvincing, and I saw the whole of "unscripted television" tip its hand in that moment — the carfucker wasn't real, the premise of the show was a sham, and none of this actually existed outside the purview of some writers' room somewhere. The world, in that moment, became so much easier to understand. More recently, I was randomly surfing and came upon an online discussion of a recent "Black Mirror" episode, and the comments section was stuffed with people arguing about the plot points in the episode as though they were real events that had actually happened, and not as a ham-fisted technology metaphor for exploring various facets of the human condition. And it was at that point at it seemed that the whole of the "social content" space tipped its hand — I knew I was watching LLMs pretend to argue with other LLMs about a work of fiction, in some bizarre ouroboros of make-believe, all ostensibly for the purpose increasing "engagement", and making it look like there was peer consensus around this kind of thing. And now I wonder how much of this place is just bots propping up the carcass. I have noticed a theme of "all the real users left a while ago" in some comments, maybe the LLMs are even saying the quiet part out loud?
item.dc:creator: 0xDEC0DE

item.title: It was intense
item.dc:creator: Shenaniganz80

item.title: THE DAM ON THE WRONG SIDE
item.description: THE DAM ON THE WRONG SIDE (A Lullaby for the Liars) Every stone, every brick, every whisper laid thick— a wall of pretend, a truthless parade, stacked high with the lies they made. They built and built, with sharpened pride, but they were building the dam on the wrong side. Yes— they were building the dam on the wrong side. Some say, “Repeat a lie, and people will believe it.” But I heard the truth in the cracks beneath it. I saw too much. Heard what they couldn’t name— noises that came from thoughts, from dusk, from the double-headed eagle’s flame. It came in music. In poems left behind. In empty libraries, where silence had a spine. And me— I was hanging from the chandelier, wishing to curl up inside a flower, drink some dew, sleep through their power. But I found strength. And I came back. I ate every book, tore through every track— looking for answers, only to learn the answers had always burned their way into me. There are no lies in the universe. Only echoes. Only seeing. And the more they tried to hide, the more we started feeling. We know where your cemeteries lie. We know how you chained the light. You tarnished innocence with your grief, but angels don’t die. You thought you were clever— stacking the bodies, sealing the flood, sacrificing purity to keep your hands clean of blood. But you were building the dam on the wrong side. Yes— you were building the dam on the wrong side. And now the river comes, not with war, but with memory. With music you can’t silence. With eyes you can’t meet. With children humming tunes from places you dared mistreat. The flood doesn’t scream. It sees. It knows. It presses. And now, you feel it— not crashing, but confessing. Because the dam is cracking, not from the front, but from inside. You were never holding back the water. You were just the ones who couldn’t see the tide. You built it up with all your might. With fear, and steel, and genocidal pride. But you were building the dam on the wrong side. Again, and again— on the wrong side. Poem by Vesp - Artwork by Litvinova Julia fallen angels with bound wings 2016
item.dc:creator: Multipass

item.title: Happy Meme Monday from The Litterbox! [OC]
item.description:
Hubby and I use the "Matthew McConaughey Smoking" reaction image a lot, so I thought it would be funny (and useful to us!) to have Litterbox versions!
I paired it up with the "It wasn't Chocolate" text from a few years back! Oh my goodness, I was out getting my wisdom teeth pulled at the time and I felt like the lucky one! (:
By Stratus on Litterbox Discord Join at https://litterbox.gg/
By riksoz on Litterbox Discord Join at https://litterbox.gg/
By YouCanCallMeKnuckles on Litterbox Discord Join at https://litterbox.gg/
BLANK #1
BLANK #2
item.dc:creator: LitterboxComics

item.title: It's a bit of a running gag at this point
item.description: My mother will use every name in the book except yours for some reason before eventually getting it, but it's still impressive that she defaulted to feminine names despite being wrong.
item.dc:creator: MelodyLightfoot

item.title: Confection Barred
item.description: So the story goes, there I was working a new job at 7 11. It was time to throw away the old stale pastries, so I took the stale ones back to the dish dishwashing area. Well, being that I was poor, and subsequently hungry, I thought “why not, it’s just trash”. I didn’t really think about it, (while knowing all too well it was against company policy) I ate a bite IN FRONT OF the other new-higher. I gotta call the next morning saying that I was fired for stealing from the company. And this is my confession: I hope you’re satisfied Stephanie —but really, I hope (if you’re every poor and hungry) that you fucking choke on a stale fucking donut. THE END
item.dc:creator: pinkmilkyway

item.title: The best little helpers
item.description: These disposable litters boxes are the best, and I’m putting together a bunch now so it’s easier to swap them out. The kittens are *almost* exclusively using the litter boxes so the time is coming when I’ll be able to let them out into the kitten room!
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1358 (all tendies)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
Sorry about the shitty cropping in today's edition.
Honestly? Both. Both are fine, fascinating, wonderful women. I would love to get to know either of them better, and i would let them sack and burn me if they wished it. Or extort me for land in Hispania, whatever.
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "Hi Pippin. Poor baby. Hey baby. Pickle. Pickle pickle. Good boy." [Whipsered while destroying a box and then while bouncing to the top of my head.] (He picked up "Pickle Pickle pickle" from my friend Kayla's ring-neck. This amuses me.) ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! If you're a woman or minority in a STEM field in Arizona living north of Phoenix and you need new headshots for this website or another detailing your experiences/research/accomplishment because our monstrous regime has deleted your experience from an official government website, PM me. Pippin and I have a good Nikon DSLR, and we'll do the photos for free, and, uh, you'll get to meet Pippin, I guess? (If you know someone who could use this who's not on Imgur, feel free to PM me to get my email address for them and tell them they're free to message me.) 3) Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 4) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 5) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett
